metadata.title: Five-Question Surface Router
metadata.description_blurb: Before you touch the keyboard, five sixty-second questions tell you which Claude surface fits the work. Miss one and you're asking Chat to handle a folder of twelve PDFs.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B07: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: Most people open the Claude surface closest to hand. They reach for Chat because Chat is already open. They open Code because Code is already running. But before you touch the keyboard, five sixty-second questions tell you which surface fits the work. Miss one, and you are asking Chat to handle a folder of twelve PDFs. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, the five-question sprint

props.command: Which Claude surface should I use to assemble a grant report from 12 PDFs in a folder?

beats.narration_text: Question one: what type of output does this task produce? Text output goes to Chat. Code goes to Code. A file — a report, a spreadsheet, a renamed folder — goes to Cowork. A decision that affects someone else's work goes to a human, not an AI surface.
beats.new_visual_element: Output type routing table — Text→Chat / Code→Code / File→Cowork / Decision→Human

beats.narration_text: Question two: where does the context live? Files already on disk point you to Cowork — not Chat. A repo points you to Code. What is in your head or sitting in your clipboard stays in Chat. The surface follows the data, not the habit.
beats.new_visual_element: Context location diagram — Files on disk → Cowork / Repo → Code / Head+clipboard → Chat

beats.narration_text: Questions three and four: risk and reversibility. Low complexity can carry high privacy risk. Healthcare case notes are not difficult to summarize — they are regulated. Assess both dimensions before you open any surface. Risk is not difficulty.
beats.new_visual_element: Risk ≠ difficulty callout — healthcare case-notes example

beats.narration_text: Question five ends the sprint. Can you describe exactly how you would verify the output? Draw the verification path in one sentence. If you cannot draw it — if no check exists — do not delegate the task. That is the whole rule.
beats.new_visual_element: No verification path = no delegation — the one rule

beats.narration_text: Five questions, sixty seconds, one routing decision. Output type, context location, risk, reversibility, verification path. Run them in order. The surface that survives all five is the right one.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — five questions on one page, routing table

beats.narration_text: Run it yourself. Take this prompt, paste it into Claude before your next task, and find out which surface fits before you start.
beats.new_visual_element: HANDOFF — paste-ready prompt for the viewer

props.command: I need to assemble a grant report from 12 PDFs in a shared folder. Run the five routing questions — output type, context location, risk, reversibility, verification path — and tell me which Claude surface fits this task.

beats.narration_text: If this saved you from a bad routing call, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
